content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content:
U moet het wachtwoord dat is ingesteld voor uw mail gebruiken. gebruikt u wel eens webmail van uw provider? dan is het wachtwoord wat u daar gebruikt om in te loggen het wachtwoord dat u moet invoeren.

U kunt ook op uw pc (als daar de mail goed werkt) in thunderbird gaan naar instellingen => privacy & beveiliging => opgeslagen wachtwoorden. Als u bij de eerste installatie van thunderbird heeft aangegeven dat het wachtwoord moest worden opgeslagen kunt u het daar vinden

content:
Hello there Ash721

Yes, Welcome ,it s possible to bring your own creations to thunderbird too. I should look for these two languages if they do

Yes, Thunderbird supports the Marathi language and the Devanagari script.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Hello there Ash721


In Thunderbird, the user interface language (UI) and the spell checker are two completely separate systems that can be configured independently
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Hello there


Interface: Go to Settings > General > Language. Click Search for more languages to add Marathi. Restart Thunderbird to apply. Spell Check: Go to the Thunderbird Dictionary Page, download the Marathi dictionary, and install it via Add-ons and Themes. Usage: Right-click in a new email compose window to switch the spell-check language to Marathi.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Hello there Ash721


Using utf-8 / 9 Yes, Devanagari (the script used for Hindi, Marathi, Nepali, and Sanskrit) is supported in Mozilla Thunderbird. While it is a "complex script" (utilizing diacritics and ligatures), modern versions of Thunderbird support it similarly to other languages using Unicode (UTF-8).

Set to UTF-8: Go to Tools > Options > Display > Formatting > Advanced and set encoding to Unicode (UTF-8).

Before using it in Thunderbird, ensure your operating system (Windows/Linux/Mac) has a Devanagari keyboard layout added (e.g., Hindi Phonetic or Inscript).

On the pc Try to install the selected language on the computer to make it more stable. Both languages are having complex characters.

Tip Avoid outdated text styles to prevent character corruption and scrambled data.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

creator: lyle.beaman

content:
So, is the little star moving to your desired account, but then when you reopen Thunderbird, it's back to the Gmail account? And the "Make Default" button's grayed-out property reverts as well?

Do you notice any other preferences not sticking? I ask because the Default Account designation is stored in the prefs.js file, located in your Profile. If one preference is not sticking, I would expect to see other properties not sticking, either.

First thing to try is to restart Thunderbird in Troubleshooting Mode. Go to menu item <Help | Troubleshoot Mode...>. Let it temporarily, not permanently, disable all your add-ons. After it restarts, try changing the default account again. If it's successful, that could indicate a misbehaving add-on.

Please let us know the results. If that doesn't do it there are some other things to try.
creator: rednoise

content: was it showing the date you last closed Thunderbird as the date modified? the prefs.js is written on the successful closing of the application. Not the taking down of the last visible window. The shut down of the application. I am increasingly seeing folk that close all the windows and Thunderbird does not close because some other software on their system is keeping it alive (mostly antivirus products, but not exclusively). Check in Windows task manager (ctrl+Shift+Esc) that there is no Thunderbird application and no Thunderbird background process as that is what it becomes when the last window is pulled down still running.
